mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth over a 5-year rolling window, or if the average return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate below a certain threshold signals a tightening labor market, which can lead to wage inflation and eventually higher corporate costs, negatively impacting future earnings and stock prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is below 5.5% over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy relative to inflation, which typically supports equity valuations as real rates are low, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with accommodative policy
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances, or if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant in predicting returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) in a low-interest-rate environment signal robust economic activity and consumer confidence, leading to short-term positive market sentiment and S&P 500 equity returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return when HOUST > 1000.0 and FEDFUNDS < 1.0 is not significantly positive (p-value > 0.1) over a 7-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive long-term S&P 500 equity performance over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns of SPX show a negative or zero correlation with the 3-month annualized growth rate of PAYEMS over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below a critical level signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall economic activity, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of instances when INDPRO shows a 3-month annualized decline greater than 1% over a 15-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong but retail sales (RSAFS) are decelerating, it suggests a potential divergence where overall economic output is not translating into robust consumer spending, which could be a leading indicator of future economic weakness and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 1-year growth > 2% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 1%
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or if the interaction effect between GDPC1 and RSAFS is not statistically significant in predicting negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) during a period of low inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals an unexpected tightening by the central bank, which can shock markets and lead to short-term negative S&P 500 equity returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return is positive when FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.1% in a month while CPIAUCSL 1-year growth is below 2%, over a 10-year period.
